Notice Of Motion For Terminating Sanction Dismissing The Action And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Nov-05-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Defendant Boas International Motors, Inc.'s Motion For Terminating Sanction Dismissing The Action And For Monetary Sanctions is granted. No opposition has been filed. The court finds that additional orders to appear for deposition would not result in compliance. The court finds that Plaintiff's failure to comply with the court's order was a misuse of the discovery process. Monetary sanctions are awarded against Plaintiff in the amount of $560, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

Ntc Of Mtn For Order Compelling Pltf To Attend, Testify, And Produce Documents At Deposition And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Aug-23-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Jessica Fakhimi, Esq., appeared for Defendant & opposing party: Boas International Motors, Inc.
Matthew Lakota, Plaintiff in Propria Persona appeared via telephone.

Ruling:
Hearing held on Defendant Boas International Motors, Inc.'s Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Attend, Testify, And Produce Documents At Deposition And For Monetary Sanctions.

RULING AFTER HEARING: Plaintiff is ordered to appear for deposition on 9/10/07 at 10 AM and to produce documents as follows:
No.1 - Denied.
Nos. 2, 3, and 4 - Granted, not including and all medical records, but sufficient to establish the physical disability. The placard and conclusory driver's statements of eligibility are not sufficient. Nos. 5 and 8 are denied. Nos. 6 and 7 are granted. Further notice of order waived. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

Motion To Limit The Scope Of Discovery And Protective Order
Off Calendar Aug-23-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearance:
Matthew Lakota, Plaintiff in Propria Persona appeared via telephone.
Jessica Fakhimi, Esq., appeared for Defendant & opposing party: Boas International Motors, Inc.

Ruling:
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff Matthew Lakota's Motion To Limit The Scope Of Discovery And Protective Order is denied. The court will not determine the scope of discovery except as that determination is based upon specific requests. Defendant's request for sanctions is denied. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
